pattern: Since width depends only on the smallest and largest chosen values, sorting lets each element be counted by how often it is the maximum minus how often it is the minimum across all subsets, collapsing an exponential sum to a closed form with powers of two.
hints: Order is irrelevant to a subset's width, so sort first; then each value's contribution is determined purely by its rank.
A value at sorted position i is the maximum of the 2^i subsets drawn from everything below it and the minimum of the 2^(n-1-i) subsets drawn from everything above it.
Sum value[i] * (2^i - 2^(n-1-i)) over all i, taking every operation modulo 1000000007 and precomputing the powers of two.

edge_inputs.description: three distinct values out of order
all equal values give total width zero
max-range pair to exercise the modulus

stress.description: moderate random arrays with duplicates so contributions interleave
stress.note: kept to hundreds so the O(n^2) oracle stays fast

stress.description: narrow value range to force many ties
